introSection: Grand National Steeplechase was a steel racing roller coaster previously located at Pirate's World in Dania, Florida, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Although some sources claim that the ride originally opened in 1897, that is incorrect - a similar ride, Steeplechase, opened in 1897 but was destroyed by fire in 1907. This ride opened as part of the re-built Steeplechase Park at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA on May 17, 1908.

The ride closed on September 20, 1964. It was relocated to Pirate's World, where it opened in 1967.

One of the ride's four tracks was removed at some point during its operation at Pirate's World.

fullText:
Grand National Steeplechase was a steel racing roller coaster previously located at Pirate's World in Dania, Florida, USA.

Although some sources claim that the ride originally opened in 1897, that is incorrect - a similar ride, Steeplechase, opened in 1897 but was destroyed by fire in 1907. This ride opened as part of the re-built Steeplechase Park at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA on May 17, 1908.

The ride closed on September 20, 1964. It was relocated to Pirate's World, where it opened in 1967.

One of the ride's four tracks was removed at some point during its operation at Pirate's World.
